preferences: User prefers Python type hints on all function signatures; no bare Exception catches.
User prefers async/await patterns over threading for I/O operations.
stable_facts: Primary runtime environment is nanobot workspace on Linux.
Memory backend is mem0 with local qdrant storage.
Caching layer uses Redis with 1GB maxmemory.
Infrastructure managed by Terraform; CI/CD via GitHub Actions.
Knowledge graph backed by Neo4j 5.x in Docker.
Production SLA target is 99.9% uptime with 5-minute incident response.
Config schemas use Pydantic v2; migration from v1 in progress.
active_projects: Memory relevance lift rollout is in progress.
Agent configuration remains separated from runtime state under ~/.nanobot.
Knowledge graph integration with Neo4j entity ontology.
Cross-encoder reranker integration for improved retrieval precision.
relationships: Carlos collaborates with platform and infrastructure teams.
Decisions needing user input are escalated directly to Carlos.
Carlos leads the nanobot project and reports to the engineering director.
Alice is on the platform team and raises performance tickets.
Bob manages the infrastructure team and handles on-call rotations.
Carlos and Bob collaborated on the Redis incident post-mortem.
constraints: Commands must avoid destructive operations unless explicitly requested.
Use env > ~/.nanobot/config.json > code defaults for memory rollout config.
All database schema migrations must be backward-compatible for zero-downtime deploys.
Must validate all user inputs before shell execution.
